snippets.title: Memoization — pay O(n) memory to drop O(2ⁿ) to O(n)
snippets.explanation: One decorator, one tradeoff: O(n) cache buys you exponential time savings.

snippets.title: Hash index — pay O(n) memory for O(1) lookups
snippets.code:
stopwords = [...]                # 50k items
stop_set = set(stopwords)         # O(n) build, O(n) memory

for token in stream:
    if token in stop_set:         # O(1) average vs O(n) on list
        continue
snippets.explanation: Building the set once (O(n) time, O(n) memory) replaces an O(n) scan per lookup. Net win at any non-trivial query count.

snippets.title: Prefix sums — pay O(n) memory for O(1) range queries
snippets.explanation: One-time O(n) construction; every range query is O(1). The right trade when many queries are coming.

snippets.title: Streaming — pay O(n) wall-clock for O(1) memory
snippets.explanation: Streaming is no faster, but lets you process datasets that don't fit in RAM.

snippets.title: Rolling rows in DP — pay nothing, save O(m)
snippets.explanation: Because each row depends only on the previous, you can drop memory from O(m·n) to O(n) at no time cost — pure space optimization.

snippets.title: Caching with TTL — pay (size · TTL) memory for hit-rate
snippets.explanation: Caching is the most common Gen AI tradeoff — small bounded memory for huge latency / cost wins.
